summary: Allowlist is parsed but never enforced, read-only status is incorrect, malformed allowlist entries are not handled safely, and wallet addresses are logged in cleartext.

issues.description: Allowlist is defined but never enforced at message send time, enabling unauthorized authenticated wallets to send messages despite CHAT_ALLOWED_ADDRESSES being set.
issues.explanation: This enforces allowlist authorization on every chat message, preventing bypass via reconnection or message manipulation, and ensures only valid Ethereum addresses are matched.

issues.description: Welcome message marks readOnly solely based on authentication, so allowlisted gating is invisible to clients and violates read-only UX requirement for non-allowlisted users.
issues.explanation: Clients now immediately know if they are in read-only mode due to allowlist restrictions, satisfying the UX requirement.

issues.description: Malformed or empty CHAT_ALLOWED_ADDRESSES entries are silently included in the allowlist, enabling incorrect matching and making configuration brittle.
issues.explanation: Only valid Ethereum addresses are accepted into the allowlist, and empty/malformed entries are ignored safely to avoid unexpected behavior.

issues.description: Wallet addresses are logged in cleartext, violating the requirement to avoid leaking addresses in logs.
issues.explanation: Redacting wallet addresses prevents sensitive identifiers from being leaked in logs while preserving traceability.
